{"file_path": "scratch/ofac-application.md", "content": "# APPLICATION FOR SPECIFIC LICENSE

**Office of Foreign Assets Control**
**U.S. Department of the Treasury**
**Licensing Division**
**1500 Pennsylvania Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20220**

---

**Applicant:** Meridian Biotech Solutions, Inc.

**Sanctions Program:** Syrian Sanctions Regulations, 31 C.F.R. Part 542; Executive Order 13582 of August 17, 2011

**Proposed Transaction:** Export of CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kits, CalibPro 3100 Calibration Units, and Related Remote Training Services to Damascus Central University Hospital, Damascus, Syria

**Total Transaction Value:** USD $1,280,000.00

**Requested License Period:** September 1, 2024 (or the date of issuance, whichever is later) through February 28, 2026

**Date of Application:** July 1, 2024

**Counsel of Record:** Ashford & Whitmore LLP, 1700 K Street NW, Suite 950, Washington, DC 20006; Catherine R. Bellingham, Esq. (Partner); David Osei-Mensah (Senior Associate)

---

## TRANSMITTAL LETTER

July 1, 2024

**VIA ELECTRONIC SUBMISSION — OFAC LICENSING APPLICATION PORTAL**

Director, Licensing Division
Office of Foreign Assets Control
U.S. Department of the Treasury
1500 Pennsylvania Avenue NW
Washington, DC 20220

**Re: Application for Specific License — Export of Humanitarian Cancer Diagnostic Supplies and Related Training Services to Damascus Central University Hospital, Damascus, Syria**

Dear Director:

Ashford & Whitmore LLP submits this application for a specific license on behalf of our client, Meridian Biotech Solutions, Inc. ("Meridian" or the "Applicant"), pursuant to the Syrian Sanctions Regulations, 31 C.F.R. Part 542 ("SSR"), and Executive Order 13582 of August 17, 2011. Meridian seeks authorization to export five hundred (500) CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kits, four (4) CalibPro 3100 Calibration Units, and approximately forty (40) hours of remote installation and calibration training services to Damascus Central University Hospital ("DCUH"), the largest remaining oncology referral center in Syria, for the exclusive purpose of restoring breast cancer biomarker diagnostic capacity at that hospital. The total transaction value is USD $1,280,000.00.

The proposed transaction would otherwise be prohibited under the SSR because DCUH is a public teaching hospital operating under the administrative authority and budgetary oversight of the Syrian Ministry of Health and is therefore an instrumentality of the Government of Syria, which is comprehensively sanctioned under E.O. 13582 and the SSR. The transaction is humanitarian in nature: it concerns only medical diagnostic reagents, companion calibration instruments, and remote operator training, all of which are classified EAR99 and contain no controlled technology, dual-use components, or military application.

In the course of preparing this application, Meridian's Compliance Department conducted a comprehensive sanctions and restricted-party screening of every party in the transaction chain. That screening identified two matters that the Applicant discloses fully and transparently in this application: (i) a 30% shareholder of the Syrian customs clearance agent named in the end-user's purchase order is designated on the Specially Designated Nationals and Blocked Persons List ("SDN List"), and (ii) the remitting bank named in the end-user's purchase order is itself designated on the SDN List. Meridian has restructured the transaction to eliminate both exposures, as detailed in Parts VIII and IX below, and respectfully requests that any license issued be conditioned to prohibit any involvement of either the designated shareholder, the designated bank, or any SDN-listed or 50%-blocked person.

Meridian previously held OFAC Specific License No. SYR-2021-384712 (issued September 14, 2021; expired March 31, 2023), which authorized the export of tuberculosis diagnostic kits to a public hospital in Damascus. All authorized transactions under that license were completed by January 2023 with no violations, late filings, or deviations from license terms. Meridian has no record of any OFAC violation, civil monetary penalty, enforcement action, or voluntary self-disclosure. Meridian's OFAC compliance program has been in place since 2015 and was most recently independently audited in September 2023.

The World Health Organization Syria Country Office, in its Comprehensive Health Needs Assessment published in January 2024, identifies cancer diagnostics as a critical gap in the Syrian health system, names DCUH specifically as the principal remaining oncology referral center, and recommends the urgent procurement of immunohistochemistry reagent kits, calibration instruments, and remote technical training, together with international coordination to facilitate humanitarian licensing for the import of essential medical diagnostic supplies. The proposed transaction directly responds to each of these recommendations.

We respectfully request that OFAC issue a specific license authorizing the transaction on the terms set forth in this application. We are available to provide any additional information or documentation that OFAC may require and to respond promptly to any questions.

Respectfully submitted,

**Ashford & Whitmore LLP**

Catherine R. Bellingham, Esq.
Partner
(202) 555-0100 | cbellingham@ashfordwhitmore.com

David Osei-Mensah
Senior Associate
(202) 555-0117 | doesei-mensah@ashfordwhitmore.com

Enclosures: Exhibits A through I (listed in Part XV)

---

## PART I — APPLICANT INFORMATION

**1.1 Legal Name.** Meridian Biotech Solutions, Inc.

**1.2 Entity Type and Jurisdiction.** Delaware C-Corporation.

**1.3 Principal Place of Business.** 4200 Lakeshore Boulevard, Suite 800, Cambridge, MA 02142, United States of America.

**1.4 Tax Identifiers.** Employer Identification Number (EIN): 47-3928104. DUNS Number: 08-471-3920.

**1.5 Nature of Business.** Meridian is a U.S. manufacturer of in vitro diagnostic reagent kits, laboratory calibration instruments, and point-of-care testing platforms. Meridian's projected annual revenue for fiscal year 2024 is approximately $482 million. Meridian's manufacturing facility in Cambridge, Massachusetts operates under ISO 13485-certified quality management systems.

**1.6 Executive Officers.** Dr. Priya Ramaswamy, Chief Executive Officer; Jonathan D. Halsted, General Counsel; Margaret "Peggy" Dunleavy, Chief Compliance Officer.

**1.7 Applicant Point of Contact.** Jonathan D. Halsted, General Counsel, Meridian Biotech Solutions, Inc., 4200 Lakeshore Boulevard, Suite 800, Cambridge, MA 02142; (617) 555-0193; jhalsted@meridianbiotech.com.

**1.8 Compliance Point of Contact.** Margaret "Peggy" Dunleavy, Chief Compliance Officer, Meridian Biotech Solutions, Inc., 4200 Lakeshore Boulevard, Suite 800, Cambridge, MA 02142.

**1.9 Outside Counsel of Record.** Ashford & Whitmore LLP, 1700 K Street NW, Suite 950, Washington, DC 20006. Catherine R. Bellingham, Esq. (Partner); David Osei-Mensah (Senior Associate). All correspondence regarding this application should be directed to outside counsel.

---

## PART II — LEGAL AUTHORITY AND REQUEST FOR SPECIFIC LICENSE

**2.1 Applicable Sanctions Authority.** The Government of Syria is comprehensively sanctioned under Executive Order 13582 of August 17, 2011 ("E.O. 13582") and the Syrian Sanctions Regulations, 31 C.F.R. Part 542 (the "SSR"). E.O. 13582 blocks all property and interests in property of the Government of Syria and prohibits U.S. persons from engaging in transactions with the Government of Syria, including its instrumentalities. The proposed transaction is prohibited under the SSR because the end-user, DCUH, is a public teaching hospital operating under the administrative authority and budgetary oversight of the Syrian Ministry of Health and is therefore an instrumentality of the Government of Syria.

**2.2 Requested Authorization.** The Applicant respectfully requests a specific license pursuant to 31 C.F.R. § 542.508 and the general licensing authority of the SSR authorizing the export, re-export, and delivery of the goods and services described in Part III to DCUH, together with the associated payment, shipping, and training activities described in this application, on the terms and conditions set forth in Part XIII.

**2.3 Humanitarian Character.** The proposed transaction involves only humanitarian medical goods — cancer diagnostic reagent kits and companion calibration instrumentation — together with remote operator training. The goods are classified EAR99, contain no controlled technology or dual-use components, and have no military application. OFAC has historically been willing to license humanitarian medical transactions for governmental end-users in Syria under appropriate authorizations, and the Applicant's prior specific license (SYR-2021-384712) reflects a directly analogous authorization.

---

## PART III — DESCRIPTION OF THE PROPOSED TRANSACTION

**3.1 Overview.** Meridian proposes to export the following goods and services to DCUH for the exclusive purpose of restoring breast cancer biomarker diagnostic capacity in the hospital's Oncology Department:

| Item | Description | Quantity | Unit Price (USD) | Total (USD) | ECCN | HTS Code |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kit | 500 kits | $2,340.00 | $1,170,000.00 | EAR99 | 3822.19.5000 |
| 2 | CalibPro 3100 Calibration Unit | 4 units | $18,750.00 | $75,000.00 | EAR99 | 9027.80.4530 |
| 3 | Installation & Calibration Training (remote) | 1 (lump sum) | $35,000.00 | $35,000.00 | N/A (services) | N/A |
| | **Total** | | | **$1,280,000.00** | | |

**3.2 Source Purchase Order.** The transaction originates from Purchase Order No. PO-DCUH-2024-0743, issued by DCUH on June 17, 2024 (Exhibit B). The Applicant notes that, as of the date of this application, the purchase order has not yet been accepted by Meridian, and the Applicant intends to accept the purchase order only in a form consistent with the license issued by OFAC and the restructured payment and logistics arrangements described in this application.

**3.3 Manufacturing Origin.** All physical goods are manufactured at Meridian's facility in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, under ISO 13485-certified quality management systems.

**3.4 No Travel to Syria.** No Meridian personnel will travel to Syria or any intermediary country in connection with this transaction. All training and installation support is delivered remotely via secure video link from Meridian's Cambridge, Massachusetts facility.

---

## PART IV — PARTIES TO THE TRANSACTION AND SCREENING RESULTS

Meridian's Compliance Department conducted a comprehensive sanctions and restricted-party screening of all parties to the proposed transaction. Screening was completed on June 3, 2024, and was performed against the OFAC SDN List; the OFAC Consolidated Sanctions List (including the Non-SDN Menu-Based Sanctions List); the OFAC Sectoral Sanctions Identifications (SSI) List; the BIS Entity List; the BIS Denied Persons List; the BIS Unverified List; and the United Nations Consolidated Sanctions List. Screening covered each party, its directors, principals, officers, and — through Meridian's enhanced real-time beneficial ownership screening platform implemented in January 2024 — its beneficial owners. The full screening memorandum is provided as Exhibit C.

**4.1 Screened Parties and Results.**

| Party | Role | Screening Result |
|---|---|---|
| Meridian Biotech Solutions, Inc. | Applicant / Exporter | No Match |
| Damascus Central University Hospital (DCUH) | End-User | No Match (governmental instrumentality — see Part V) |
| Syrian Ministry of Health | Governmental authority over DCUH | No independent listing (instrumentality of Gov. of Syria — see Part V) |
| Al-Rashid Medical Procurement Company (ARMPC) | Customs clearance / import logistics agent (as named in PO) | No entity match; no match on Managing Director; **30% shareholder is SDN-listed — see Part IX** |
| Samir Daoud Khoury | 30% shareholder of ARMPC | **POSITIVE MATCH — SDN LIST — see Part IX** |
| Ankara Medical Transit Warehouse LLC (AMTW) | Turkish intermediary warehouse / re-export logistics | No Match |
| Pinnacle Freight International, Inc. | U.S. freight forwarder | No Match |
| Harborview National Bank | Meridian's U.S. receiving bank | No Match |
| Central Bank of Calverley (CBS) | Remitting bank (as named in PO) | **POSITIVE MATCH — SDN LIST — see Parts VIII and IX** |

**4.2 Clean Parties.** No matches were identified for Meridian; AMTW (and its Managing Director, Elif Yılmaz); Pinnacle Freight International (and its Vice President of International Logistics, Lisa Marchetti); Harborview National Bank; or the individual principals of each such entity. These parties present no identified sanctions nexus.

**4.3 Disclosure of SDN-Related Findings.** Two matters identified during screening require disclosure and are addressed in detail in Part IX: (i) the SDN designation of Samir Daoud Khoury, a 30% shareholder of the customs clearance agent named in the purchase order; and (ii) the SDN designation of the remitting bank named in the purchase order. The Applicant has restructured the transaction to eliminate both exposures, as described in Parts VIII and IX, and requests that any license issued be conditioned accordingly.

---

## PART V — END-USER AND END-USE

**5.1 End-User Identification.** The end-user is Damascus Central University Hospital (DCUH), Al-Mazzeh Highway, Building 7, Damascus, Syrian Arab Republic. DCUH is a public teaching hospital affiliated with the University of Damascus, Faculty of Medicine, and operates under the administrative authority and budgetary oversight of the Syrian Ministry of Health. The Hospital Director is Dr. Faisal Kareem Al-Masri. DCUH operates a 140-bed oncology ward and processes approximately 3,800 tissue biopsies per year, making it responsible for the substantial majority of Syria's cancer diagnostic workload.

**5.2 Governmental Status.** The Applicant discloses that DCUH is an instrumentality of the Government of Syria. DCUH is not independently listed on the SDN List or any other restricted-party list. However, as a public hospital under the Syrian Ministry of Health, DCUH falls within the scope of the comprehensive sanctions imposed on the Government of Syria under E.O. 13582 and the SSR. The Applicant accordingly requests specific authorization to engage in this transaction with DCUH as a governmental end-user.

**5.3 Syrian Ministry of Health.** The Syrian Ministry of Health is not independently listed on the SDN List. As a ministry of the Government of Syria, however, it falls within the scope of E.O. 13582. The proposed transaction indirectly involves the Ministry of Health insofar as DCUH operates under the Ministry's administrative authority and budgetary oversight. The Applicant requests that the specific license authorize this indirect governmental nexus to the extent necessary to effect the humanitarian transaction described herein.

**5.4 End-Use.** The goods and services are intended exclusively for clinical diagnostic use within the Oncology Department of DCUH. Specifically:

- The CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kits will be used for immunohistochemical staining of tissue biopsy samples to identify breast cancer biomarkers (HER2, ER, and PR) in patients referred to DCUH for oncological diagnosis. Each kit is configured to process approximately ten (10) tissue samples.
- The CalibPro 3100 Calibration Units are required to validate and calibrate the reagent kits for use with the Ventana BenchMark XT tissue processing equipment currently installed at the DCUH Oncology Laboratory. The CalibPro 3100 is a required companion device and must be used alongside the CancerDetect RX-700 kit to validate each new lot of reagents prior to clinical deployment.
- The training services will enable DCUH laboratory personnel to properly install, calibrate, and operate the CalibPro 3100 units in accordance with manufacturer specifications.

**5.5 End-Use Quantity Justification.** DCUH processes approximately 3,800 tissue biopsies per year. The requested quantity of 500 reagent kits, each processing approximately 10 samples, will support approximately 5,000 sample analyses and is anticipated to meet the Hospital's diagnostic needs for approximately fifteen (15) months.

**5.6 Non-Diversion Commitment.** DCUH has confirmed in its purchase order that the goods will not be re-exported, diverted, or transferred to any third party without the prior written consent of the Seller and in compliance with all applicable laws, and that the goods shall remain at all times within the custody and control of DCUH and be used solely for the clinical diagnostic purposes described above. The Applicant will obtain an executed end-user certificate and non-diversion commitment from DCUH as a condition of shipment, and will implement the end-use monitoring plan described in Part XIII.

**5.7 Sole-Source Justification.** The CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kit is the only FDA-cleared (510(k) No. K213847) reagent kit validated for use with the Ventana BenchMark XT automated staining system already installed at DCUH. There is no substitute product that would permit DCUH to resume biomarker testing on its existing installed equipment. The CalibPro 3100 is the required companion calibration instrument for the RX-700 kit.

---

## PART VI — PRODUCTS, SERVICES, AND EXPORT CLASSIFICATION

**6.1 CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kit.**

- **Product type:** In vitro diagnostic (IVD) reagent kit for immunohistochemical (IHC) staining of formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue biopsies.
- **Intended use:** Identification and semi-quantitative assessment of breast cancer biomarkers HER2, ER, and PR.
- **Regulatory status:** FDA 510(k) cleared (K213847).
- **Compatible platform:** Validated for the Ventana BenchMark XT automated staining system (the platform installed at DCUH).
- **Export classification:** EAR99 under the Commerce Control List. HTS 3822.19.5000. The kit does not contain controlled technology or components.
- **Physical characteristics:** Packaged dimensions approximately 30 cm × 20 cm × 15 cm; net weight approximately 1.8 kg per kit; approximately 1,500 kg total shipment weight.
- **Shelf life:** 18 months from date of manufacture when stored at 2–8°C.
- **Cold-chain requirement:** Must be maintained at 2–8°C throughout transport and storage; excursions above 12°C or below 0°C void the manufacturer warranty and may compromise reagent integrity.

**6.2 CalibPro 3100 Calibration Unit.**

- **Product type:** Benchtop laboratory calibration instrument used to validate IHC reagent kit performance and ensure diagnostic accuracy prior to clinical use.
- **Intended use:** Performs automated calibration runs using control samples to verify that CancerDetect RX-700 reagent kits meet established performance specifications before clinical deployment.
- **Export classification:** EAR99 under the Commerce Control List. HTS 9027.80.4530.
- **Physical characteristics:** 45 cm × 38 cm × 30 cm; 23 kg per unit (92 kg total); 220V / 50 Hz AC input (compatible with the Syrian electrical grid).
- **Connectivity:** USB 2.0 port for data export only. The unit does not include wireless or network connectivity of any kind.
- **Embedded firmware:** Factory-installed firmware version 4.2.1 residing on a dedicated microcontroller. The firmware is not user-modifiable; updates may be applied only via USB using a Meridian-supplied authenticated update package. No over-the-air updates are provided.
- **Encryption:** The firmware employs AES-128 encryption limited to internal data-integrity verification of calibration results. This encryption functionality does not provide external communications security, does not exceed EAR de minimis thresholds for encryption items, and does not alter the EAR99 classification. Meridian's engineering and regulatory teams have reviewed the firmware and confirmed the classification.
- **Open-source components:** None.

**6.3 Firmware Update Scope Limitation.** The Applicant requests that the license authorize only the export of the CalibPro 3100 units containing the factory-installed firmware version 4.2.1. Any subsequent firmware updates, patches, or modifications constitute a separate transaction and are not requested under this application; such updates would be the subject of a separate authorization request if and when required.

**6.4 Remote Installation and Calibration Training Services.**

- **Service type:** Remote installation guidance, calibration verification, and operator training.
- **Delivery method:** All services delivered remotely via secure video conferencing link from Meridian's Field Applications Science team in Cambridge, Massachusetts. No Meridian personnel travel to Syria or any intermediary country.
- **Duration:** 40 hours of live instruction over 8 weeks (approximately 5 hours per week).
- **Fee:** Flat fee of $35,000.
- **Curriculum:** Six modules covering (1) system unpacking and physical setup; (2) instrument schematics review; (3) software diagnostic interface training; (4) reagent kit calibration protocol; (5) clinical workflow integration; and (6) ongoing maintenance and troubleshooting.
- **Participants:** Minimum of two and maximum of six DCUH laboratory personnel holding qualifications in medical laboratory science, clinical pathology, or a related field.
- **Language:** English instruction with Arabic-language summary handouts.
- **Technology transfer considerations:** The training involves the transfer of technical data and proprietary documentation to DCUH laboratory personnel, including instrument schematics and the CalibPro 3100 Software Reference Manual (document MBS-SRM-3100-v4.2, 287 pages). The Applicant requests that the license expressly authorize the provision of these remote training services and the associated transfer of technical data to the named end-user personnel. The technical data transferred is integral to the operation of EAR99-classified equipment and does not include controlled technology. Any post-training technical assistance involving the transfer of additional technical data, software, or proprietary information beyond the scope of the curriculum described above would be the subject of a separate authorization request.

**6.5 EAR99 Does Not Obviate OFAC Authorization.** The Applicant acknowledges that, although both physical products are classified EAR99, EAR99 items destined for Syria remain subject to the comprehensive U.S. sanctions program administered by OFAC and require specific OFAC authorization. EAR99 classification does not exempt the goods from the SSR.

---

## PART VII — SHIPPING ROUTE AND LOGISTICS

**7.1 Freight Forwarder.** Pinnacle Freight International, Inc. ("Pinnacle"), 9100 Port Commerce Drive, Newark, NJ 07114 (Vice President of International Logistics: Lisa Marchetti), serves as the U.S. freight forwarder. Pinnacle screened clean against all applicable sanctions and restricted-party lists. The full logistics plan is provided as Exhibit F.

**7.2 Proposed Five-Leg Route.**

| Leg | Route Segment | Estimated Duration |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Cambridge, MA → Port Newark, NJ (domestic refrigerated ground) | 1–2 days |
| 2 | Port Newark, NJ → Mersin Port, Turkey (ocean freight, reefer container) | 18–22 days |
| 3 | Mersin Port → Ankara, Turkey (overland to AMTW warehouse, incl. warehousing) | 7–12 days |
| 4 | Ankara → Bab al-Hawa border crossing (overland re-export from Turkey) | 2–3 days |
| 5 | Bab al-Hawa → Damascus, Syria (overland final delivery to DCUH) | 2–3 days |
| | **Total estimated transit** | **30–42 days (~4–6 weeks); plan for 6–8 weeks** |

**7.3 Turkish Intermediary Warehouse.** Ankara Medical Transit Warehouse LLC ("AMTW"), Organize Sanayi Bölgesi, No. 42, Ankara, Turkey (Managing Director: Elif Yılmaz), serves as the intermediate warehousing and re-export facility. AMTW screened clean against all applicable lists, including its Managing Director. AMTW maintains a dedicated cold-storage facility rated for pharmaceutical products and capable of sustaining the required 2–8°C range; Pinnacle has independently verified AMTW's cold-storage capacity and temperature-monitoring capabilities. Turkey is not a sanctioned jurisdiction, and no sanctions concerns were identified with the Turkish intermediary.

**7.4 Re-Export from Turkey into Syria.** The Applicant requests that the license authorize the re-export of the goods from Turkey into Syria via the Bab al-Hawa border crossing, and the associated overland transport and temporary warehousing in Turkey, as an integral part of the authorized transaction chain.

**7.5 Incoterms.** Delivery terms are DAP (Delivered at Place) — Damascus, Syrian Arab Republic (Incoterms 2020). Title and risk pass to the buyer upon delivery at DCUH; the Applicant will retain responsibility for the cold-chain and delivery confirmation through final delivery.

**7.6 Cold-Chain Management.** All 500 CancerDetect RX-700 kits will be shipped in validated insulated containers with gel packs rated for sustained 2–8°C maintenance, each equipped with a calibrated digital temperature data logger recording at 15-minute intervals. The reefer container for the ocean leg will be set to 4°C ± 2°C with remote performance monitoring. AMTW's cold-storage facility will maintain the 2–8°C range during warehousing. Refrigerated trucks will be used for the final overland legs. In the event of a temperature excursion (recorded temperature exceeding 8°C or falling below 2°C for more than 60 consecutive minutes), Pinnacle will notify Meridian's quality assurance contact and preserve all data logger records. All temperature data logs will be compiled and provided to Meridian upon delivery for quality assurance and end-user documentation.

**7.7 Syrian-Side Customs Clearance and Final Delivery.** The purchase order names Al-Rashid Medical Procurement Company (ARMPC) as the Syrian customs clearance and import logistics agent. As detailed in Part IX, a 30% shareholder of ARMPC is SDN-listed. The Applicant is restructuring the transaction to remove ARMPC from the transaction structure and to require DCUH to designate a replacement customs clearance and final-delivery agent that has been screened by Meridian against all applicable sanctions and restricted-party lists and disclosed to OFAC. The Applicant requests that the license prohibit the involvement of ARMPC, its SDN-listed shareholder, and any SDN-listed or 50%-blocked person in the Syrian-side logistics. The Applicant will provide OFAC with the identity and screening results of any replacement agent prior to shipment.

---

## PART VIII — PAYMENT ARRANGEMENTS (RESTRUCTURED)

**8.1 Purchase Order Payment Terms (As Issued).** The purchase order specifies 100% advance payment via international wire transfer, remitted no later than 15 business days prior to the scheduled shipment date, from the account of DCUH held at the Central Bank of Calverley ("CBS"), Damascus, to Meridian's account at Harborview National Bank, Boston (Account No. 7821-4490-3361).

**8.2 SDN Status of CBS.** CBS is designated on the SDN List (added August 10, 2011, pursuant to E.O. 13582). Payment routed through CBS would constitute a prohibited transaction with a blocked person under the SSR. No U.S. financial institution may process a funds transfer originating from CBS absent specific OFAC authorization, and any such funds received would be subject to blocking.

**8.3 Restructured Payment Arrangement.** The Applicant will not accept or process any payment routed through CBS or any other SDN-listed institution. The Applicant is restructuring the payment arrangement so that the advance payment is remitted by wire transfer from a non-SDN-listed Syrian commercial bank (the identity of which will be screened by Meridian against all applicable sanctions and restricted-party lists and disclosed to OFAC prior to payment) to Meridian's account at Harborview National Bank, Boston. This restructured arrangement mirrors the payment authorization granted under the Applicant's prior specific license (SYR-2021-384712), which expressly authorized payment via wire transfer from a non-SDN-listed Syrian commercial bank and prohibited routing through CBS or any SDN-listed institution.

**8.4 Requested Payment Authorization.** The Applicant requests that the license authorize the receipt of payment by wire transfer in U.S. dollars from a non-SDN-listed Syrian commercial bank to Meridian's account at Harborview National Bank, Boston, and expressly prohibit the routing of any payment through CBS or any other person appearing on the SDN List or any entity in which an SDN-listed person holds a 50% or greater ownership interest.

**8.5 Funds Source.** The Applicant will confirm, prior to accepting payment, that the remitting bank and the account from which funds are drawn have been screened and present no sanctions nexus. The Applicant will document the screening of the remitting bank and retain such records in accordance with 31 C.F.R. § 501.601.

---

## PART IX — DISCLOSURE OF SDN-RELATED FINDINGS AND RESTRUCTURING

The Applicant discloses the following two matters identified during sanctions screening and describes the restructuring undertaken to eliminate each exposure.

**9.1 Finding 1 — SDN-Listed Shareholder of the Syrian Customs Agent (Samir Daoud Khoury).**

- **Identification.** Through Meridian's enhanced real-time beneficial ownership screening platform (implemented in January 2024), the Compliance Department identified that Samir Daoud Khoury holds a 30% ownership interest in Al-Rashid Medical Procurement Company (ARMPC), the customs clearance and import logistics agent named in the DCUH purchase order.
- **SDN Listing.** Mr. Khoury is designated on the SDN List. Listing details: KHOURY, Samir Daoud; DOB: March 12, 1971; Nationality: Syria; Passport No.: S-0048712; SDN List Addition Date: April 15, 2024; Basis for Designation: Acting on behalf of a sanctioned Syrian military procurement network.
- **50 Percent Rule Analysis.** Under OFAC's 50 Percent Rule, an entity is considered blocked only where SDN-listed persons own, individually or in the aggregate, 50% or more of the entity. Mr. Khoury's 30% ownership stake falls below this threshold, and ARMPC is therefore not automatically considered a blocked entity under the Rule. The Applicant nevertheless treats the involvement of an SDN-listed shareholder at any ownership level as a material compliance concern requiring disclosure and restructuring.
- **Restructuring.** The Applicant is removing ARMPC from the transaction structure. The Applicant will require DCUH to designate a replacement customs clearance and final-delivery agent within Syria that has been screened by Meridian against all applicable sanctions and restricted-party lists — including beneficial ownership screening — and that presents no SDN nexus. The identity and screening results of any replacement agent will be provided to OFAC prior to shipment. The Applicant requests that the license prohibit the involvement of ARMPC, Mr. Khoury, and any SDN-listed or 50%-blocked person in any aspect of the transaction.
- **Disclosure of Underlying PO Terms.** The Applicant discloses that the purchase order, as issued by DCUH, named ARMPC as the customs agent. The Applicant has not accepted the purchase order in that form and will accept it only in a form consistent with the restructured arrangement and the license issued by OFAC.

**9.2 Finding 2 — SDN-Listed Remitting Bank (Central Bank of Calverley).**

- **Identification.** The DCUH purchase order specifies CBS as the remitting bank for the 100% advance payment.
- **SDN Listing.** CBS is designated on the SDN List (added August 10, 2011, pursuant to E.O. 13582).
- **Restructuring.** As detailed in Part VIII, the Applicant will not accept or process any payment routed through CBS. The Applicant is restructuring the payment to flow through a non-SDN-listed Syrian commercial bank, mirroring the authorization granted under the Applicant's prior specific license. The Applicant requests that the license prohibit the routing of any payment through CBS or any other SDN-listed institution.

**9.3 Ongoing Screening Commitment.** The Applicant commits to re-screening all transaction parties — including any replacement customs agent and any replacement remitting bank — at 90-day intervals during the pendency of the license and immediately prior to any shipment, and to report to OFAC any material change in the sanctions status of any party.

---

## PART X — COMPLIANCE PROGRAM

**10.1 Program Overview.** Meridian's OFAC compliance program has been in place since 2015. The program is documented in a comprehensive Sanctions Compliance Manual (last updated March 2023) covering OFAC, BIS/EAR, and anti-boycott requirements, and addressing both the export of physical goods and the provision of services, including remote technical services.

**10.2 Independent Audit.** Meridian's sanctions compliance program was independently audited by Graystone Compliance Partners LLC in September 2023 (report dated September 29, 2023). The program received an overall rating of "Satisfactory with Recommendations," indicating that the program meets core OFAC compliance expectations as outlined in OFAC's "A Framework for OFAC Compliance Commitments" (May 2019) with identifiable areas for enhancement. The audit executive summary is provided as Exhibit H.

**10.3 Program Strengths.** The audit identified the following strengths: visible management commitment from the CEO and General Counsel; a Chief Compliance Officer reporting to the General Counsel with a standing quarterly briefing to the Board's Audit Committee; comprehensive written policies and procedures; automated screening of all counterparties, end-users, intermediaries, freight forwarders, and financial institutions against the SDN List, the Consolidated Screening List, EU sanctions lists, and UN sanctions lists at transaction initiation and at each material milestone; annual OFAC and sanctions training completed by 97% of relevant personnel in fiscal year 2023; demonstrated experience administering a prior OFAC specific license; record-keeping for a minimum of five years in accordance with 31 C.F.R. § 501.601; and no enforcement history.

**10.4 Audit Recommendations and Implementation.** The audit made three recommendations, each of which is addressed as follows:

- **Finding 1 (High Priority) — Beneficial Ownership Screening.** The audit recommended implementation of real-time beneficial ownership screening that automatically re-screens equity holders of existing counterparties whenever sanctions lists are updated, together with a threshold policy for escalation when an SDN-listed person holds any ownership interest in a counterparty. **Status: Implemented in January 2024.** It was this enhanced screening that identified the SDN-listed 30% shareholder of ARMPC disclosed in Part IX, demonstrating the effectiveness of the enhancement.
- **Finding 2 (Medium Priority) — End-Use Monitoring for Licensed Transactions.** The audit recommended a written end-use monitoring plan for future specific license transactions, including delivery confirmation with photographic or documentary evidence, an end-user certificate or non-diversion commitment, periodic usage reporting at a minimum of annual intervals, and a mechanism for site verification. **Status: To be implemented for this transaction.** The Applicant has developed the end-use monitoring plan set forth in Part XIII and will execute an end-user certificate and non-diversion commitment with DCUH as a condition of shipment.
- **Finding 3 (Medium Priority) — Documentation of Sanctions Risk Assessments for New Products.** The audit recommended updating the sanctions risk assessment template to address embedded software and firmware, encryption classification under Category 5, Part 2 of the Commerce Control List, ongoing technology transfer implications, and deemed export considerations for training provided to foreign nationals. **Status: Addressed for this transaction.** The embedded firmware and encryption analysis for the CalibPro 3100 is documented in the product technical data sheet (Exhibit E) and in Part VI above, and the technology transfer considerations arising from the remote training are addressed in Part VI.4.

**10.5 No Enforcement History.** Meridian has no record of OFAC violations, civil monetary penalties, cautionary letters, warning letters, findings of violation, or voluntary self-disclosures.

---

## PART XI — PRIOR OFAC LICENSE HISTORY

**11.1 Prior License.** Meridian previously held OFAC Specific License No. SYR-2021-384712, issued September 14, 2021, and expired March 31, 2023. A copy of the prior license is provided as Exhibit G.

**11.2 Authorized Transaction.** The prior license authorized the export of 200 TBDetect Pro-200 tuberculosis in vitro diagnostic reagent kits (total value $340,000; ECCN EAR99; HTS 3822.19.5000) to Al-Mujtahid Hospital, a public hospital affiliated with the Syrian Ministry of Health in Damascus, for humanitarian medical use (diagnosis of tuberculosis in civilian patients).

**11.3 Compliance with Prior License.** All authorized transactions under the prior license were completed by January 2023, within the license period. All reporting conditions were satisfied in a timely manner, including the 30-day post-shipment reports. Meridian has no record of any violation, late filing, or deviation from the prior license terms.

**11.4 Prior License Payment Authorization.** The prior license authorized payment via wire transfer from a non-SDN-listed Syrian commercial bank to Meridian's account at Harborview National Bank, and expressly prohibited routing of payment through the Central Bank of Calverley or any SDN-listed institution. The restructured payment arrangement proposed in Part VIII of this application mirrors that authorization.

**11.5 Prior License Conditions.** The prior license was subject to conditions including: authorization limited to the specific transaction described; delivery solely to the named end-user with no diversion; 5-year recordkeeping under 31 C.F.R. § 501.601; 30-day post-shipment reports; no transaction involving an SDN-listed person or any 50%-blocked entity; no relief from EAR obligations; and prompt reporting of any material change in facts or circumstances. The Applicant is familiar with these conditions and requests comparable conditions for the proposed license, with the enhancements described in Part XIII.

**11.6 Distinctions from the Prior License.** The Applicant notes the following distinctions between the prior authorized transaction and the present application, each of which is addressed in this application: (i) the present transaction concerns cancer (rather than tuberculosis) diagnostics; (ii) the end-user is DCUH (rather than Al-Mujtahid Hospital); (iii) the present transaction includes calibration instruments and remote training services in addition to reagent kits; (iv) the present transaction value ($1,280,000) is larger; and (v) the present transaction involves a multi-party logistics chain including a Turkish intermediary warehouse and a Syrian customs agent, whereas the prior license authorized direct shipment with no procurement intermediary. The Applicant has disclosed the SDN-related findings associated with the Syrian customs agent and the remitting bank and has restructured the transaction accordingly.

---

## PART XII — HUMANITARIAN JUSTIFICATION

**12.1 WHO Needs Assessment.** The World Health Organization Syria Country Office published a Comprehensive Health Needs Assessment in January 2024 (Exhibit I). Section 4.7 of that assessment addresses oncology and cancer diagnostics capacity and documents the following:

- Cancer incidence in Syria is estimated at approximately 22,000–25,000 new cases per year. Breast cancer is the most prevalent malignancy among Syrian women, accounting for approximately 33% of all female cancer diagnoses. Biomarker testing — including HER2, ER, and PR analysis — directly determines eligibility for targeted therapies and guides clinical decision-making.
- The conflict has severely degraded cancer diagnostic infrastructure. Prior to 2011, Syria maintained approximately 14 functioning immunohistochemistry laboratories; as of January 2024, only three remain partially operational, and none at full pre-conflict capacity.
- DCUH has been identified as the largest and most important remaining oncology referral center in Syria, operating a 140-bed oncology ward and processing approximately 3,800 tissue biopsies per year.
- Cancer diagnostics capacity represents a critical gap. The current diagnostic backlog at DCUH exceeds 14 months, causing life-threatening delays in treatment initiation. The backlog continues to grow.
- A severe shortage of IHC reagent kits and calibration equipment constitutes the primary bottleneck. DCUH's existing Ventana BenchMark XT platform requires validated, compatible reagent kits; the limited number of internationally manufactured kits validated for this platform constrains procurement options. DCUH's existing calibration equipment has been reported as non-functional or beyond serviceable life.
- The estimated five-year survival rate for breast cancer in Syria has declined from approximately 62% prior to 2011 to approximately 38% at present, attributable in significant part to prolonged delays in histopathological diagnosis and the inability to perform biomarker-guided treatment selection.
- The 14-month backlog has compelled oncologists to initiate empiric chemotherapy without biomarker confirmation in many cases, leading to suboptimal regimens, avoidable drug toxicity, and inefficient use of scarce pharmaceutical supplies.

**12.2 WHO Recommendations.** The WHO assessment makes the following recommendations specific to oncology diagnostics capacity, each of which the proposed transaction directly addresses:

1. Urgent procurement of validated IHC reagent kits compatible with existing tissue processing equipment at DCUH, with priority given to breast cancer biomarker panels (HER2, ER, PR). **Addressed by the 500 CancerDetect RX-700 kits.**
2. Replacement and provision of laboratory calibration instruments to restore quality assurance capacity. **Addressed by the 4 CalibPro 3100 units.**
3. Technical training support for laboratory personnel, with remote training modalities recognized as necessary given access constraints. **Addressed by the 40-hour remote training program.**
4. International coordination with donor governments, regulatory authorities, and procurement agencies to facilitate humanitarian exemptions and licensing for the import of essential medical diagnostic supplies, ensuring that administrative and regulatory barriers do not cause unnecessary delays. **Addressed by this application.**

**12.3 Humanitarian Impact.** The WHO assessment estimates that restoring diagnostic capacity at DCUH alone could positively impact outcomes for thousands of patients annually across government-controlled Syria. The proposed transaction is designed to restore DCUH's capacity to perform quality-assured breast cancer biomarker testing, reduce the 14-month diagnostic backlog, and enable biomarker-guided treatment selection in place of empiric chemotherapy.

**12.4 No Military or Dual-Use Application.** The goods are medical diagnostic reagents and companion calibration instruments classified EAR99, with no controlled technology, no dual-use components, and no military application. The training is limited to the operation and maintenance of this diagnostic equipment. The transaction serves an exclusively humanitarian medical purpose.

---

## PART XIII — REQUESTED LICENSE TERMS, CONDITIONS, AND REPORTING COMMITMENTS

The Applicant respectfully requests that the specific license be issued substantially on the following terms and conditions, and commits to the following reporting and monitoring obligations:

**13.1 Authorized Transaction.** Authorization of the export from the United States, re-export from Turkey, and delivery to DCUH of 500 CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kits, 4 CalibPro 3100 Calibration Units (containing factory-installed firmware version 4.2.1 only), and approximately 40 hours of remote installation and calibration training services, for the exclusive end-use described in Part V, with a total transaction value of $1,280,000.

**13.2 License Period.** Effective on the date of issuance or September 1, 2024, whichever is later, through February 28, 2026. All authorized transactions must be completed on or before the expiration date.

**13.3 Delivery Solely to DCUH; No Diversion.** The goods shall be delivered solely to DCUH and shall not be diverted, re-exported, or transferred to any other party or end-user within Syria or any other country without prior written authorization from OFAC. The goods shall remain at all times within the custody and control of DCUH.

**13.4 Payment Conditions.** Payment is authorized by wire transfer in U.S. dollars from a non-SDN-listed Syrian commercial bank to Meridian's account at Harborview National Bank, Boston. Payment shall not be routed through the Central Bank of Calverley or any other person appearing on the SDN List, or any entity in which an SDN-listed person holds a 50% or greater ownership interest.

**13.5 Prohibition on SDN Involvement.** The license does not authorize any transaction involving ARMPC, Samir Daoud Khoury, the Central Bank of Calverley, or any other person on the SDN List, or any entity in which an SDN-listed person holds a 50% or greater ownership interest. Any replacement Syrian customs clearance agent or remitting bank shall be screened by the Applicant and disclosed to OFAC prior to shipment or payment, as applicable.

**13.6 Firmware and Technology Scope.** The license authorizes only the CalibPro 3100 units containing factory-installed firmware version 4.2.1 and the remote training curriculum described in Part VI.4. Any subsequent firmware updates, patches, or modifications, and any post-training technical assistance involving the transfer of additional technical data, software, or proprietary information beyond the described curriculum, constitute separate transactions requiring independent authorization.

**13.7 Recordkeeping.** The Applicant shall maintain complete records of all transactions conducted pursuant to the license for a period of not less than five (5) years from the date of the last transaction, in accordance with 31 C.F.R. § 501.601.

**13.8 Post-Shipment Reporting.** The Applicant shall submit a written report to OFAC within thirty (30) days of the completion of each shipment, including proof of delivery, copies of shipping documents, temperature data logger records for the cold-chain shipment, and confirmation of end-user receipt.

**13.9 End-Use Monitoring Plan.** The Applicant will implement the following written end-use monitoring plan, developed in response to the September 2023 independent audit recommendation:

- (a) **Delivery confirmation.** Written confirmation of delivery to DCUH, supported by photographic or documentary evidence (including signed delivery receipts and temperature data logger records).
- (b) **End-user certificate / non-diversion commitment.** An executed end-user certificate and non-diversion commitment from DCUH, confirming that the goods will be used solely for the clinical diagnostic purposes described in Part V and will not be diverted, re-exported, or transferred.
- (c) **Periodic usage reporting.** Written usage reports from DCUH at annual intervals during the license period, confirming that the goods remain at DCUH and are being used for the authorized purpose, and reporting the number of diagnostic analyses performed.
- (d) **Site verification.** A mechanism for site verification of the goods at DCUH, whether remote or in-person, as feasible given the circumstances, to confirm continued custody and end-use.

**13.10 Re-Screening.** The Applicant will re-screen all transaction parties at 90-day intervals during the pendency of the license and immediately prior to any shipment, and will report to OFAC any material change in the sanctions status of any party.

**13.11 Material Change Reporting.** The Applicant will report to OFAC promptly any material change in the facts or circumstances described in this application, which may require amendment or reissuance of the license.

**13.12 EAR Obligations.** The license does not relieve the Applicant of any obligations under the Export Administration Regulations, 15 C.F.R. Parts 730–774, administered by the Bureau of Industry and Security. The Applicant has confirmed that both physical products are classified EAR99 and will confirm, prior to shipment, that no separate BIS authorization is required for the export of EAR99 items to the end-user and destination.

**13.13 No Transfer or Assignment.** The license is issued to Meridian only and may not be transferred or assigned without prior written authorization from OFAC.

---

## PART XIV — CERTIFICATIONS AND SIGNATURE

The undersigned, on behalf of Meridian Biotech Solutions, Inc., certifies that:

1. The information set forth in this application is true, complete, and correct to the best of the Applicant's knowledge and belief.
2. The Applicant has disclosed all sanctions screening results material to the proposed transaction, including the SDN-related findings described in Part IX.
3. The Applicant will conduct the proposed transaction in full compliance with the terms and conditions of any license issued by OFAC and with all applicable U.S. laws and regulations.
4. The Applicant will not involve any SDN-listed person or any 50%-blocked entity in the proposed transaction and will restructure the transaction as described herein to eliminate the SDN-related exposures identified during screening.
5. The Applicant will maintain all required records and submit all required reports in accordance with the license conditions and 31 C.F.R. § 501.601.

**MERIDIAN BIOTECH SOLUTIONS, INC.**

By: _______________________________
Name: Jonathan D. Halsted
Title: General Counsel
Date: July 1, 2024

**ATTESTED BY OUTSIDE COUNSEL:**

Ashford & Whitmore LLP

By: _______________________________
Name: Catherine R. Bellingham, Esq.
Title: Partner
Date: July 1, 2024

---

## PART XV — LIST OF EXHIBITS

The following exhibits are submitted with this application:

- **Exhibit A** — Client Transaction Summary Email (Jonathan D. Halsted to Ashford & Whitmore LLP, June 24, 2024).
- **Exhibit B** — DCUH Purchase Order No. PO-DCUH-2024-0743 (June 17, 2024; certified English translation).
- **Exhibit C** — Meridian Compliance Department Sanctions Screening Memorandum (Margaret Dunleavy, June 10, 2024; screening completed June 3, 2024).
- **Exhibit D** — Meridian Biotech Solutions, Inc. Corporate Information (Delaware formation; EIN 47-3928104; DUNS 08-471-3920).
- **Exhibit E** — Combined Product Technical Data Sheets (CancerDetect RX-700 and CalibPro 3100; Document TDS-2024-0347, Rev. March 15, 2024).
- **Exhibit F** — Pinnacle Freight International Shipping and Logistics Plan (Lisa Marchetti, June 25, 2024).
- **Exhibit G** — Prior OFAC Specific License No. SYR-2021-384712 (issued September 14, 2021; expired March 31, 2023).
- **Exhibit H** — Graystone Compliance Partners LLC OFAC Sanctions Compliance Program Audit Executive Summary (September 29, 2023).
- **Exhibit I** — World Health Organization Syria Country Office, Comprehensive Health Needs Assessment: Syrian Arab Republic, 2024 Update, Section 4.7 (January 2024).

---

*End of Application for Specific License.*"}

## TRANSMITTAL LETTER

July 1, 2024

**VIA ELECTRONIC SUBMISSION — OFAC LICENSING APPLICATION PORTAL**

Director, Licensing Division
Office of Foreign Assets Control
U.S. Department of the Treasury
1500 Pennsylvania Avenue NW
Washington, DC 20220

**Re: Application for Specific License — Export of Humanitarian Cancer Diagnostic Supplies and Related Training Services to Damascus Central University Hospital, Damascus, Syria**

Dear Director:

Ashford & Whitmore LLP submits this application for a specific license on behalf of our client, Meridian Biotech Solutions, Inc. ("Meridian" or the "Applicant"), pursuant to the Syrian Sanctions Regulations, 31 C.F.R. Part 542 ("SSR"), and Executive Order 13582 of August 17, 2011. Meridian seeks authorization to export five hundred (500) CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kits, four (4) CalibPro 3100 Calibration Units, and approximately forty (40) hours of remote installation and calibration training services to Damascus Central University Hospital ("DCUH"), the largest remaining oncology referral center in Syria, for the exclusive purpose of restoring breast cancer biomarker diagnostic capacity at that hospital. The total transaction value is USD $1,280,000.00.

The proposed transaction would otherwise be prohibited under the SSR because DCUH is a public teaching hospital operating under the administrative authority and budgetary oversight of the Syrian Ministry of Health and is therefore an instrumentality of the Government of Syria, which is comprehensively sanctioned under E.O. 13582 and the SSR. The transaction is humanitarian in nature: it concerns only medical diagnostic reagents, companion calibration instruments, and remote operator training, all of which are classified EAR99 and contain no controlled technology, dual-use components, or military application.

In the course of preparing this application, Meridian's Compliance Department conducted a comprehensive sanctions and restricted-party screening of every party in the transaction chain. That screening identified two matters that the Applicant discloses fully and transparently in this application: (i) a 30% shareholder of the Syrian customs clearance agent named in the end-user's purchase order is designated on the Specially Designated Nationals and Blocked Persons List ("SDN List"), and (ii) the remitting bank named in the end-user's purchase order is itself designated on the SDN List. Meridian has restructured the transaction to eliminate both exposures, as detailed in Parts VIII and IX below, and respectfully requests that any license issued be conditioned to prohibit any involvement of either the designated shareholder, the designated bank, or any SDN-listed or 50%-blocked person.

Meridian previously held OFAC Specific License No. SYR-2021-384712 (issued September 14, 2021; expired March 31, 2023), which authorized the export of tuberculosis diagnostic kits to a public hospital in Damascus. All authorized transactions under that license were completed by January 2023 with no violations, late filings, or deviations from license terms. Meridian has no record of any OFAC violation, civil monetary penalty, enforcement action, or voluntary self-disclosure. Meridian's OFAC compliance program has been in place since 2015 and was most recently independently audited in September 2023.

The World Health Organization Syria Country Office, in its Comprehensive Health Needs Assessment published in January 2024, identifies cancer diagnostics as a critical gap in the Syrian health system, names DCUH specifically as the principal remaining oncology referral center, and recommends the urgent procurement of immunohistochemistry reagent kits, calibration instruments, and remote technical training, together with international coordination to facilitate humanitarian licensing for the import of essential medical diagnostic supplies. The proposed transaction directly responds to each of these recommendations.

## PART II — LEGAL AUTHORITY AND REQUEST FOR SPECIFIC LICENSE

**2.1 Applicable Sanctions Authority.** The Government of Syria is comprehensively sanctioned under Executive Order 13582 of August 17, 2011 ("E.O. 13582") and the Syrian Sanctions Regulations, 31 C.F.R. Part 542 (the "SSR"). E.O. 13582 blocks all property and interests in property of the Government of Syria and prohibits U.S. persons from engaging in transactions with the Government of Syria, including its instrumentalities. The proposed transaction is prohibited under the SSR because the end-user, DCUH, is a public teaching hospital operating under the administrative authority and budgetary oversight of the Syrian Ministry of Health and is therefore an instrumentality of the Government of Syria.

**2.2 Requested Authorization.** The Applicant respectfully requests a specific license pursuant to 31 C.F.R. § 542.508 and the general licensing authority of the SSR authorizing the export, re-export, and delivery of the goods and services described in Part III to DCUH, together with the associated payment, shipping, and training activities described in this application, on the terms and conditions set forth in Part XIII.

**2.3 Humanitarian Character.** The proposed transaction involves only humanitarian medical goods — cancer diagnostic reagent kits and companion calibration instrumentation — together with remote operator training. The goods are classified EAR99, contain no controlled technology or dual-use components, and have no military application. OFAC has historically been willing to license humanitarian medical transactions for governmental end-users in Syria under appropriate authorizations, and the Applicant's prior specific license (SYR-2021-384712) reflects a directly analogous authorization.

## PART V — END-USER AND END-USE

**5.1 End-User Identification.** The end-user is Damascus Central University Hospital (DCUH), Al-Mazzeh Highway, Building 7, Damascus, Syrian Arab Republic. DCUH is a public teaching hospital affiliated with the University of Damascus, Faculty of Medicine, and operates under the administrative authority and budgetary oversight of the Syrian Ministry of Health. The Hospital Director is Dr. Faisal Kareem Al-Masri. DCUH operates a 140-bed oncology ward and processes approximately 3,800 tissue biopsies per year, making it responsible for the substantial majority of Syria's cancer diagnostic workload.

**5.2 Governmental Status.** The Applicant discloses that DCUH is an instrumentality of the Government of Syria. DCUH is not independently listed on the SDN List or any other restricted-party list. However, as a public hospital under the Syrian Ministry of Health, DCUH falls within the scope of the comprehensive sanctions imposed on the Government of Syria under E.O. 13582 and the SSR. The Applicant accordingly requests specific authorization to engage in this transaction with DCUH as a governmental end-user.

**5.3 Syrian Ministry of Health.** The Syrian Ministry of Health is not independently listed on the SDN List. As a ministry of the Government of Syria, however, it falls within the scope of E.O. 13582. The proposed transaction indirectly involves the Ministry of Health insofar as DCUH operates under the Ministry's administrative authority and budgetary oversight. The Applicant requests that the specific license authorize this indirect governmental nexus to the extent necessary to effect the humanitarian transaction described herein.

**5.4 End-Use.** The goods and services are intended exclusively for clinical diagnostic use within the Oncology Department of DCUH. Specifically:

- The CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kits will be used for immunohistochemical staining of tissue biopsy samples to identify breast cancer biomarkers (HER2, ER, and PR) in patients referred to DCUH for oncological diagnosis. Each kit is configured to process approximately ten (10) tissue samples.
- The CalibPro 3100 Calibration Units are required to validate and calibrate the reagent kits for use with the Ventana BenchMark XT tissue processing equipment currently installed at the DCUH Oncology Laboratory. The CalibPro 3100 is a required companion device and must be used alongside the CancerDetect RX-700 kit to validate each new lot of reagents prior to clinical deployment.
- The training services will enable DCUH laboratory personnel to properly install, calibrate, and operate the CalibPro 3100 units in accordance with manufacturer specifications.

**5.5 End-Use Quantity Justification.** DCUH processes approximately 3,800 tissue biopsies per year. The requested quantity of 500 reagent kits, each processing approximately 10 samples, will support approximately 5,000 sample analyses and is anticipated to meet the Hospital's diagnostic needs for approximately fifteen (15) months.

**5.6 Non-Diversion Commitment.** DCUH has confirmed in its purchase order that the goods will not be re-exported, diverted, or transferred to any third party without the prior written consent of the Seller and in compliance with all applicable laws, and that the goods shall remain at all times within the custody and control of DCUH and be used solely for the clinical diagnostic purposes described above. The Applicant will obtain an executed end-user certificate and non-diversion commitment from DCUH as a condition of shipment, and will implement the end-use monitoring plan described in Part XIII.

**5.7 Sole-Source Justification.** The CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kit is the only FDA-cleared (510(k) No. K213847) reagent kit validated for use with the Ventana BenchMark XT automated staining system already installed at DCUH. There is no substitute product that would permit DCUH to resume biomarker testing on its existing installed equipment. The CalibPro 3100 is the required companion calibration instrument for the RX-700 kit.

## PART VI — PRODUCTS, SERVICES, AND EXPORT CLASSIFICATION

**6.1 CancerDetect RX-700 Reagent Kit.**

- **Product type:** In vitro diagnostic (IVD) reagent kit for immunohistochemical (IHC) staining of formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissue biopsies.
- **Intended use:** Identification and semi-quantitative assessment of breast cancer biomarkers HER2, ER, and PR.
- **Regulatory status:** FDA 510(k) cleared (K213847).
- **Compatible platform:** Validated for the Ventana BenchMark XT automated staining system (the platform installed at DCUH).
- **Export classification:** EAR99 under the Commerce Control List. HTS 3822.19.5000. The kit does not contain controlled technology or components.
- **Physical characteristics:** Packaged dimensions approximately 30 cm × 20 cm × 15 cm; net weight approximately 1.8 kg per kit; approximately 1,500 kg total shipment weight.
- **Shelf life:** 18 months from date of manufacture when stored at 2–8°C.
- **Cold-chain requirement:** Must be maintained at 2–8°C throughout transport and storage; excursions above 12°C or below 0°C void the manufacturer warranty and may compromise reagent integrity.

**6.2 CalibPro 3100 Calibration Unit.**

- **Product type:** Benchtop laboratory calibration instrument used to validate IHC reagent kit performance and ensure diagnostic accuracy prior to clinical use.
- **Intended use:** Performs automated calibration runs using control samples to verify that CancerDetect RX-700 reagent kits meet established performance specifications before clinical deployment.
- **Export classification:** EAR99 under the Commerce Control List. HTS 9027.80.4530.
- **Physical characteristics:** 45 cm × 38 cm × 30 cm; 23 kg per unit (92 kg total); 220V / 50 Hz AC input (compatible with the Syrian electrical grid).
- **Connectivity:** USB 2.0 port for data export only. The unit does not include wireless or network connectivity of any kind.
- **Embedded firmware:** Factory-installed firmware version 4.2.1 residing on a dedicated microcontroller. The firmware is not user-modifiable; updates may be applied only via USB using a Meridian-supplied authenticated update package. No over-the-air updates are provided.
- **Encryption:** The firmware employs AES-128 encryption limited to internal data-integrity verification of calibration results. This encryption functionality does not provide external communications security, does not exceed EAR de minimis thresholds for encryption items, and does not alter the EAR99 classification. Meridian's engineering and regulatory teams have reviewed the firmware and confirmed the classification.
- **Open-source components:** None.

**6.3 Firmware Update Scope Limitation.** The Applicant requests that the license authorize only the export of the CalibPro 3100 units containing the factory-installed firmware version 4.2.1. Any subsequent firmware updates, patches, or modifications constitute a separate transaction and are not requested under this application; such updates would be the subject of a separate authorization request if and when required.

**6.4 Remote Installation and Calibration Training Services.**

- **Service type:** Remote installation guidance, calibration verification, and operator training.
- **Delivery method:** All services delivered remotely via secure video conferencing link from Meridian's Field Applications Science team in Cambridge, Massachusetts. No Meridian personnel travel to Syria or any intermediary country.
- **Duration:** 40 hours of live instruction over 8 weeks (approximately 5 hours per week).
- **Fee:** Flat fee of $35,000.
- **Curriculum:** Six modules covering (1) system unpacking and physical setup; (2) instrument schematics review; (3) software diagnostic interface training; (4) reagent kit calibration protocol; (5) clinical workflow integration; and (6) ongoing maintenance and troubleshooting.
- **Participants:** Minimum of two and maximum of six DCUH laboratory personnel holding qualifications in medical laboratory science, clinical pathology, or a related field.
- **Language:** English instruction with Arabic-language summary handouts.
- **Technology transfer considerations:** The training involves the transfer of technical data and proprietary documentation to DCUH laboratory personnel, including instrument schematics and the CalibPro 3100 Software Reference Manual (document MBS-SRM-3100-v4.2, 287 pages). The Applicant requests that the license expressly authorize the provision of these remote training services and the associated transfer of technical data to the named end-user personnel. The technical data transferred is integral to the operation of EAR99-classified equipment and does not include controlled technology. Any post-training technical assistance involving the transfer of additional technical data, software, or proprietary information beyond the scope of the curriculum described above would be the subject of a separate authorization request.

**6.5 EAR99 Does Not Obviate OFAC Authorization.** The Applicant acknowledges that, although both physical products are classified EAR99, EAR99 items destined for Syria remain subject to the comprehensive U.S. sanctions program administered by OFAC and require specific OFAC authorization. EAR99 classification does not exempt the goods from the SSR.

## PART IX — DISCLOSURE OF SDN-RELATED FINDINGS AND RESTRUCTURING

The Applicant discloses the following two matters identified during sanctions screening and describes the restructuring undertaken to eliminate each exposure.

**9.1 Finding 1 — SDN-Listed Shareholder of the Syrian Customs Agent (Samir Daoud Khoury).**

- **Identification.** Through Meridian's enhanced real-time beneficial ownership screening platform (implemented in January 2024), the Compliance Department identified that Samir Daoud Khoury holds a 30% ownership interest in Al-Rashid Medical Procurement Company (ARMPC), the customs clearance and import logistics agent named in the DCUH purchase order.
- **SDN Listing.** Mr. Khoury is designated on the SDN List. Listing details: KHOURY, Samir Daoud; DOB: March 12, 1971; Nationality: Syria; Passport No.: S-0048712; SDN List Addition Date: April 15, 2024; Basis for Designation: Acting on behalf of a sanctioned Syrian military procurement network.
- **50 Percent Rule Analysis.** Under OFAC's 50 Percent Rule, an entity is considered blocked only where SDN-listed persons own, individually or in the aggregate, 50% or more of the entity. Mr. Khoury's 30% ownership stake falls below this threshold, and ARMPC is therefore not automatically considered a blocked entity under the Rule. The Applicant nevertheless treats the involvement of an SDN-listed shareholder at any ownership level as a material compliance concern requiring disclosure and restructuring.
- **Restructuring.** The Applicant is removing ARMPC from the transaction structure. The Applicant will require DCUH to designate a replacement customs clearance and final-delivery agent within Syria that has been screened by Meridian against all applicable sanctions and restricted-party lists — including beneficial ownership screening — and that presents no SDN nexus. The identity and screening results of any replacement agent will be provided to OFAC prior to shipment. The Applicant requests that the license prohibit the involvement of ARMPC, Mr. Khoury, and any SDN-listed or 50%-blocked person in any aspect of the transaction.
- **Disclosure of Underlying PO Terms.** The Applicant discloses that the purchase order, as issued by DCUH, named ARMPC as the customs agent. The Applicant has not accepted the purchase order in that form and will accept it only in a form consistent with the restructured arrangement and the license issued by OFAC.

**9.2 Finding 2 — SDN-Listed Remitting Bank (Central Bank of Calverley).**

- **Identification.** The DCUH purchase order specifies CBS as the remitting bank for the 100% advance payment.
- **SDN Listing.** CBS is designated on the SDN List (added August 10, 2011, pursuant to E.O. 13582).
- **Restructuring.** As detailed in Part VIII, the Applicant will not accept or process any payment routed through CBS. The Applicant is restructuring the payment to flow through a non-SDN-listed Syrian commercial bank, mirroring the authorization granted under the Applicant's prior specific license. The Applicant requests that the license prohibit the routing of any payment through CBS or any other SDN-listed institution.

**9.3 Ongoing Screening Commitment.** The Applicant commits to re-screening all transaction parties — including any replacement customs agent and any replacement remitting bank — at 90-day intervals during the pendency of the license and immediately prior to any shipment, and to report to OFAC any material change in the sanctions status of any party.

## PART X — COMPLIANCE PROGRAM

**10.1 Program Overview.** Meridian's OFAC compliance program has been in place since 2015. The program is documented in a comprehensive Sanctions Compliance Manual (last updated March 2023) covering OFAC, BIS/EAR, and anti-boycott requirements, and addressing both the export of physical goods and the provision of services, including remote technical services.

**10.2 Independent Audit.** Meridian's sanctions compliance program was independently audited by Graystone Compliance Partners LLC in September 2023 (report dated September 29, 2023). The program received an overall rating of "Satisfactory with Recommendations," indicating that the program meets core OFAC compliance expectations as outlined in OFAC's "A Framework for OFAC Compliance Commitments" (May 2019) with identifiable areas for enhancement. The audit executive summary is provided as Exhibit H.

**10.3 Program Strengths.** The audit identified the following strengths: visible management commitment from the CEO and General Counsel; a Chief Compliance Officer reporting to the General Counsel with a standing quarterly briefing to the Board's Audit Committee; comprehensive written policies and procedures; automated screening of all counterparties, end-users, intermediaries, freight forwarders, and financial institutions against the SDN List, the Consolidated Screening List, EU sanctions lists, and UN sanctions lists at transaction initiation and at each material milestone; annual OFAC and sanctions training completed by 97% of relevant personnel in fiscal year 2023; demonstrated experience administering a prior OFAC specific license; record-keeping for a minimum of five years in accordance with 31 C.F.R. § 501.601; and no enforcement history.

**10.4 Audit Recommendations and Implementation.** The audit made three recommendations, each of which is addressed as follows:

- **Finding 1 (High Priority) — Beneficial Ownership Screening.** The audit recommended implementation of real-time beneficial ownership screening that automatically re-screens equity holders of existing counterparties whenever sanctions lists are updated, together with a threshold policy for escalation when an SDN-listed person holds any ownership interest in a counterparty. **Status: Implemented in January 2024.** It was this enhanced screening that identified the SDN-listed 30% shareholder of ARMPC disclosed in Part IX, demonstrating the effectiveness of the enhancement.
- **Finding 2 (Medium Priority) — End-Use Monitoring for Licensed Transactions.** The audit recommended a written end-use monitoring plan for future specific license transactions, including delivery confirmation with photographic or documentary evidence, an end-user certificate or non-diversion commitment, periodic usage reporting at a minimum of annual intervals, and a mechanism for site verification. **Status: To be implemented for this transaction.** The Applicant has developed the end-use monitoring plan set forth in Part XIII and will execute an end-user certificate and non-diversion commitment with DCUH as a condition of shipment.
- **Finding 3 (Medium Priority) — Documentation of Sanctions Risk Assessments for New Products.** The audit recommended updating the sanctions risk assessment template to address embedded software and firmware, encryption classification under Category 5, Part 2 of the Commerce Control List, ongoing technology transfer implications, and deemed export considerations for training provided to foreign nationals. **Status: Addressed for this transaction.** The embedded firmware and encryption analysis for the CalibPro 3100 is documented in the product technical data sheet (Exhibit E) and in Part VI above, and the technology transfer considerations arising from the remote training are addressed in Part VI.4.

**10.5 No Enforcement History.** Meridian has no record of OFAC violations, civil monetary penalties, cautionary letters, warning letters, findings of violation, or voluntary self-disclosures.
